sentence: Subjective:
HPI: Judy Gomez, a 61-year-old female, presents for ongoing management of psoriatic arthritis.
She reports that she has been doing well since the last visit, with the exception of occasional pain in her feet when she has a day off from her desk job.
She has been taking an extra prednisone tablet once a week to alleviate this pain.
She has been on a dose of one prednisone tablet daily and has been considering discontinuing it.
She reports that methotrexate has helped with her joint pain, but had concerns about taking it with COVID-19 vaccines, which she believes may have reduced its efficacy.
She denies taking methotrexate on the week of her first COVID-19 vaccine dose, but took it the week after and experienced severe side effects.
She reports increased bruising, particularly when bumping herself, which she attributes to the prednisone.
Past medical history: psoriatic arthritis.
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED].
Current medications: Prednisone 1 tablet daily, Methotrexate (dose and frequency not specified).
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED].
Physical exam: Examination of the patient's joints revealed tenderness in the joints of the feet and knees.
The patient had a good range of motion in the toes.
Examination of the skin revealed ridges and nail changes consistent with psoriasis.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED].
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient's psoriatic arthritis is well-managed, with the exception of occasional pain in the feet and increased bruising due to prednisone.
Problem list: Psoriatic arthritis; Prednisone-induced bruising; Methotrexate interaction with COVID-19 vaccine.
Plan:
I discontinued the patient's prednisone and instructed her to continue with the methotrexate.
I recommended that she schedule an appointment for blood work and instructed her to follow up in 3 months.
I also advised her to stop by the front desk to schedule the blood work appointment.
Follow-up:
The patient will follow up in 3 months.
